title: Logs Aggregation And Tenant Scoped Search Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the logs aggregation and tenant scoped search capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Aggregates application and audit logs into a queryable log store and exposes a tenant-scoped search contract (log search plus an observability readiness query) that admins use to investigate behaviour within a single tenant boundary. The capability is provider-backed and tenant-data-owning, so the lifecycle/readiness model must be preserved unchanged across extraction and every search request carries and enforces the tenant context.

stateModel.description: Persisted state is log entries keyed by low-cardinality service and level labels, with high-cardinality identifiers (organisation/tenant id, request id, trace id) carried as structured fields rather than labels to control series cardinality. Backing the entries is a readiness record exposing the discrete states log ingestion, tenant-scoped query, trace correlation, dashboards, collector, metrics, and error capture; schema evolution uses ordered, forward-only migrations that preserve tenant rows and audit lineage.

permissions.description: Search and readiness operations are admin-gated and hard-bound to a single tenant: every query is scoped to the caller's organisation id so one tenant can never read another tenant's log entries. Permission, audit, and fail-closed checks are treated as security-sensitive invariants that must survive extraction unchanged, and missing tenant context fails the request rather than widening scope.

contracts.description: Exposes a tenant-scoped log search operation and an observability readiness query behind ports/adapters, so the log-store provider (e.g. a Loki-style backend) is swappable without changing the contract surface. Inputs are validated and normalised before reaching the adapter, and the response shape (matched entries plus readiness state) is stable so callers and dashboards depend on semantics, not provider internals.

validation.description: Search inputs (time range, label/field filters, tenant scope) are validated for type and bounds before execution, and the mandatory tenant scope is asserted so an unscoped or cross-tenant query is rejected rather than broadened. Invariants are enforced ahead of any provider call so malformed input cannot reach the log store or leak entries outside the tenant boundary.

errorModel.description: Errors are deterministic and fail-closed: a provider or substrate failure returns a typed degraded/readiness failure instead of fabricating successful provider state or empty-but-authoritative results. Error logs carry the organisation id when tenant-scoped, mutation and side-effect paths stay closed under failure, and no partial result is presented as complete.

auditModel.description: State-changing and security-relevant actions emit audit records before the effect is acknowledged, so the audit trail cannot diverge from what actually happened. Audit entries preserve tenant boundary, request/trace correlation, and DSR/GDPR evidence requirements, and a missing tenant-isolation or audit signal is itself an alertable condition.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is a first-class, queryable posture reporting the discrete sub-states (log ingestion, tenant-scoped query, trace correlation, dashboards, collector, metrics, error capture) and degrades explicitly when the search adapter or substrate is unavailable. In degraded mode mutation/side-effect paths that depend on the search adapter are disabled while read-only readiness and audit visibility are retained; emitted readiness, error, and audit signals use the controlled, OpenTelemetry-informed observability vocabulary.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by the cited tenant-observability runtime proof plus logs use-case tests, demonstrating tenant-scoped search, readiness reporting, and fail-closed isolation as hermetic/runtime lineage rather than a current-readiness claim. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
